LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE PUNITIVE DAMAGES. RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT ESTABLISHES A PRIMA FACI BASIS FOR THE AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION FOCUSES ON WHETHER CERTAIN INDIVIDUALS WERE MANAGING AGENTS. THE DECISION IN WHITE V ULTRAMAR (1999) 21 CAL. 4TH 563 MAKES CLEAR THAT THIS IS A QUESTION OF FACT. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS CONTEST THE MEANING AND SIGNIFICANCE OF PLAINTIFF'S FACTS, THOSE FACTS CONSTITUTE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANTS' MANAGING AGENTS HAD KNOWLEDGE OF CRITICAL FACTS AND DID NOT ACT UPON. COURT RESERVES FEBRUARY 6, 2008 HEARING DATE ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF TO FILE THE MOTION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Hearing To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of An Adult With A Disability.
Off Calendar Sep-18-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of an Adult Person with a Disability.
Dept. 218, Comm. Frank J. Drago, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Terry Gross, Esq. appeared with Petitioner and Claimint.
Carla J. Hartley, Esq. appeared for defendant the Greek Orthodox Metropolis of San Francisco, a nonprofit corporation.
Paul Balestracci, Esq. appeared for defendant.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.
Order Approving the Compromise of an Adult Person with a Disability was submitted to and signed by the Court. Said Order was subsequently placed under seal pursuant to Court Order.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Complaint To Allege Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Dec-31-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
APPEARANCES: TERRY GROSS, ESQUIRE (GROSS & BELSKY, 415.544.0200) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. CARLA J. HARTLEY, ESQUIRE (DILLINGHAM & MURPHY, 415.397.2700) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE THAT ESTABLISHES A PRIMA FACI BASIS FOR THE AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION FOCUSES ON WHETHER CERTAIN INDIVIDUALS WERE MANAGING AGENTS. THE DECISION IN WHITE V ULTRAMAR (1999) 21 CAL. 4TH 563 MAKES CLEAR THAT THIS IS A QUESTION OF FACT. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANTS CONTEST THE MEANING AND SIGNIFICANCE OF PLAINTIFF'S FACTS, THOSE FACTS CONSTITUTE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANTS' MANAGING AGENTS HAD KNOWLEDGE OF CRITICAL FACTS AND DID NOT ACT UPON. COURT RESERVES FEBRUARY 6, 2008 HEARING DATE ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF TO FILE THE MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Pltf And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-10-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Terry Gross, Esq., for defendant and moving party: The Greek Orthodox Metropolis of San Francisco
Carla J. Hartley, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: John Doe

AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant The Greek Orthodox Metropolis' motion to compel a mental examination of plaintiff is Granted.
Plaintiff is ordered to submit to a further examination for the purpose of the administration of the MMPI by the psychiatrist retained by Defendant. Compliance by December 31, 2007. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-27-2007 Text Ruling

RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT AND ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING. COURT SUSTAINS PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE GLICK AND SIGAL DECLARATIONS.
